steps.title: Apply the height formula for equilateral triangles
steps.content:
For an equilateral triangle with side $s$, the height formula is $h = \frac{s\sqrt{3}}{2}$.

With height $h = 49\sqrt{3}$, substitute to get:
$$49\sqrt{3} = \frac{s\sqrt{3}}{2}$$

steps.title: Solve for the side
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by 2:
$$98\sqrt{3} = s\sqrt{3}$$
Divide both sides by $\sqrt{3}$:
$$s = \frac{98\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{3}} = 98$$
B is correct.
